title: Dry-Run Early Return Ordering for Multi-Phase Scripts
description: When implementing dry-run mode in multi-phase scripts, place the dry-run early return AFTER displaying all phase previews. Original implementation returned early before Phase 6 dry-run message, preventing users from seeing what would happen.
context: Implementing timeline automation integration into thread-export script with 6 phases. Dry-run needed to show Phase 6 preview but was returning at Phase 4.
outcome: Fixed by moving Phase 6 dry-run message BEFORE the early return statement. Now users see complete workflow preview including timeline check in dry-run mode.

title: Graceful Degradation for Enhancement Integrations
description: When integrating enhancement features into core workflows, wrap imports in try/except and check availability flag before execution. Core workflow should complete successfully even if enhancement module is unavailable.
context: Integrating timeline automation (enhancement) into thread-export (core workflow). Timeline module might not be available or could error.
outcome: Used try/except for import with TIMELINE_AVAILABLE flag. Phase 6 checks flag and wraps execution in error handler. Export completes successfully even if timeline fails.

title: Post-Archive Timeline Integration Placement
description: When integrating timeline automation into export workflows, place timeline check (Phase 6) AFTER archive creation (Phase 5) but BEFORE completion message. This ensures archive is safely written before attempting timeline updates, and timeline failures don't prevent export completion.
context: Choosing where to integrate timeline automation in 5-phase thread-export workflow. Need to balance safety with workflow coherence.
outcome: Placed as Phase 6 after archive creation. Archive is safe even if timeline fails. User gets complete view of workflow progression. Errors in Phase 6 don't roll back completed work.
